---
title: 'Research Sodas Fountain'
date: '2016-06-05T22:07:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Cornerstone Restaurants'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1390554684374595937'
blogger_filename: '/2016/06/research-sodas-fountain.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Cornerstone Restaurants/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'History'
  - 'Research'
---

<h3 style="color: #3d596d; font-family: Merriweather, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif;">
<a data-mce-href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain" href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain" style="color: #00aadc;" target="_blank">Soda Fountains become an Institution</a></h3>
<div style="color: #3d596d; font-family: Merriweather, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 15px; line-height: 25.5px; margin-bottom: 24px;">
The glory years of the “soda fountain” lasted from 1890 until the 1960s, becoming social centers for young and old alike. As gathering places, they usually had in common marble-topped counters and tables, wired-back chairs, shining mirrors, and glass and chrome serving dishes and glasses. Each, also, was presided over by the master of the ice cream soda, and the sundae – the “soda jerk,” who, if skilled enough, was as much fun to watch creating his or her masterpiece as it was in consuming it.</div>
<div style="color: #3d596d; font-family: Merriweather, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 15px; line-height: 25.5px;">
Read more at Suite101:&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain#ixzz0kqiBMssc" href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain#ixzz0kqiBMssc" style="color: #00aadc;">Brief History of the Soda Fountain: The Drug Store &amp; Ice Cream Parlor - Once Parts of American Culture</a>&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain#ixzz0kqiBMssc" href="http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain#ixzz0kqiBMssc" style="color: #00aadc;">http://americanhistory.suite101.com/article.cfm/the_origins_of_the_soda_fountain#ixzz0kqiBMssc</a></div>
